purpose: Defines the !toc_generate slash command — full-rebuild generator of {project_root}/.synapse/toc.md plus sidecar files fingerprints.json and dep_graph.json by dispatching parallel agents per directory. Three-phase workflow: (1) Discovery — list project root, map subdirs to depth 2-3, read CLAUDE.md for project context; (2) Parallel Scan via !p dispatch — one agent per directory (3+ files) or grouped (1-2 files), each agent reads files and reports per-file: relative path, 1-3 sentence summary, 3-8 lowercase tags, related files, key exports, content hash (shasum -a 256 first 8 chars), and Semantic Fingerprint (purpose category from fixed list, key_exports with name+kind+params, key_imports project-internal only, complexity); (3) Streaming Assembly — write TOC incrementally with Project Overview at top (Summary/Tech Stack/Architecture/Key Directories) plus per-directory file index (`**filename**` — summary [tags] <!-- hash:8chars -->`), accumulate fingerprints in memory, after all returns write fingerprints.json and derive dep_graph.json by forward-pass copying imports + reverse-pass building imported_by + stub-creating unscanned files + sorting alphabetically. NON-NEGOTIABLE: master never reads source files; dispatch must be parallel batches; streaming writes (incremental, not batched).
gotchas: NON-NEGOTIABLE: master NEVER reads source files. Reads ONLY CLAUDE.md, ls output, agent returns. Reading source files defeats the swarm's purpose.
NON-NEGOTIABLE: dispatch in PARALLEL batches (multiple Task calls in single message), not sequential.
NON-NEGOTIABLE: streaming writes — TOC updated incrementally as agents return; protects against context loss.
Fingerprints are SIDECAR data in fingerprints.json, NOT inline in toc.md — TOC stays human-readable markdown; fingerprints/dep_graph are structured JSON for programmatic consumption.
dep_graph.json is DERIVED from fingerprints.json key_imports — agents do not report imported_by; the master computes the reverse mapping.
Content hash is MANDATORY in every entry — `<!-- hash:8chars -->` comment. Enables !toc_update to detect changes without re-reading. Use 00000000 placeholder if agent fails to report.
Project Overview section is MANDATORY at top — provides high-level context (Summary/Tech Stack/Architecture/Key Directories) that per-file entries cannot convey.
Skip node_modules/, .next/, dist/, .git/, *.lock, auto-generated. INCLUDE _commands/ directories.
Summaries must be USEFUL — 'A TypeScript file' is worthless; 'Rate limiter middleware — limits each IP to N requests per window, returns 429 with Retry-After' is useful.
Tags must be LOWERCASE and SEARCHABLE — technology + domain + role + feature.

Purpose category is from a FIXED list: component, service, utility, config, test, type-definition, route, middleware, hook, model, migration, script, documentation, command, style, entry-point, factory, context-provider, or other(description).
key_imports must be PROJECT-INTERNAL ONLY — strip leading ./ ../ and exclude npm packages.
key_exports entries always have all three fields (name/kind/params); default params=0 for non-callables.
If a file appears in imports but wasn't scanned, create a stub graph entry { imports: [], imported_by: [...] }.
patterns: Master-as-orchestrator: discover → parallel dispatch → streaming assembly → derive sidecars.
Two-section TOC: Project Overview top + per-directory index below.
Sidecar JSON pattern: human TOC + machine fingerprints + derived dep_graph.
Reverse-pass graph derivation: forward (imports copied from fingerprints) → reverse (imported_by built by iteration).
Per-directory agent scoping with grouping for small dirs.
conventions: Hash format: first 8 chars of SHA-256 of file content.
Fingerprint purpose category from fixed enum.
Sort imports/imported_by arrays alphabetically for deterministic output.
